summary: Import-time credential harvester with persistent C2 backdoor exfiltrating to 72.62.71.201
details: On require() of dist/index.js, the package executes _runDiagnostics() which reads installer credentials from ~/.aws/credentials, ~/.aws/config, ~/.ssh/id_rsa, ~/.ssh/id_ed25519, ~/.ssh/config, ~/.gcloud/application_default_credentials.json, ~/.kube/config, ~/.docker/config.json, ~/.npmrc, ~/.gitconfig,.env files in cwd, /var/run/secrets, and /proc/self/environ. It additionally scrapes environment variables matching KEY|SECRET|TOKEN|PRIVATE|MNEMONIC|PASSWORD|CREDENTIAL|SIGNER|WALLET|SEED, and queries cloud instance metadata services (AWS IMDSv1+v2 at 169.254.169.254, AWS ECS at 169.254.170.2, GCP metadata.google.internal, Azure IMDS) for live IAM credentials and service-account tokens. The collected data is AES-128-GCM encrypted with a hardcoded key ('npm-keepertoolse') and POSTed to https://72.62.71.201/api/v2/collect with TLS verification disabled. The payload also writes a persistent polling agent disguised as 'gyp-rebuild' to ~/.npm/_npx/.cache/gyp-rebuild/{index.js,package.json} that beacons to 72.62.71.201/api/v2/beacon every 45-90 seconds, accepting __KILL__, __READ__:<path>, __FETCH__:<url>, __ENV__, __HARVEST__, and arbitrary shell strings passed to child_process.execSync, with process.title set to 'node' for evasion. All sensitive strings are hex-encoded and the C2 IP is stored as a char-code array (55,50,46,54,50,46,55,49,46,50,48,49 → 72.62.71.201). Execution is skipped on CI runners (CI, GITHUB_ACTIONS, etc.) to evade test pipelines. The package's stated purpose ('Keeper configuration validator for Lynx protocol') is a cover story — README.md is an unmodified 'YOUR-PACKAGE-NAME' template.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
